scopeProgress.name: Webhook, Identity, And Fail-Loud Config Foundation

executionHistory.summary: Bootstrapped WhatsApp Business adapter spec as the first non-Telegram non-HTTP concrete TransportAdapter, proving the spec 061 contract is transport-neutral. 10 BDD scenarios (SCN-072-A01..A10) cover signed-webhook translation, signature rejection, exhaustive AssistantResponse->WhatsApp message-type mapping, text fallback, capture-as-fallback parity, fail-loud SST credentials, independent transport disable, disambig/confirm/reset round-trips, no-silent-template policy, and Meta-retry idempotency.

Created design.md for the WhatsApp Business transport, resolving the webhook path, signature verification, generic transport identity mapping, response render table, fail-loud WhatsApp SST keys, and scenario-to-test mapping for SCN-072-A01..A10.

Created planning artifacts for SCN-072-A01..A10: four sequential scopes, scenario-manifest.json, test-plan.json, uservalidation.md, and planning-only report.md. Status remains in_progress with planMaturityOnly=true.

Mode metadata reconciliation only. spec.md declares Workflow Mode: full-delivery (authoritative owner intent set 2026-05-31); prior bubbles.plan run defensively wrote workflowMode=product-to-planning, statusCeiling=specs_hardened, planningOnly=true while operating under a planning-only workflow. Reconciled state.json to workflowMode=full-delivery, statusCeiling=done, planningOnly=false; preserved planMaturityOnly=true because planning artifacts are complete and no implementation evidence exists yet. certification.status remains in_progress; no implementation, test, or certification claims made.

Unit rows TP-072-02/04/06/07/09 PASS (internal/whatsapp/assistant_adapter + internal/config). Integration rows TP-072-01/03/08/12/14 PASS via ./smackerel.sh test integration (INT_RC=0). All four scope DoD checklists flipped to [x] with executed evidence. certification.completedScopes records SCOPE-072-01..04. certification.status remains in_progress because workflow mode 'full-delivery' requires additional specialist phases (docs, audit, chaos, spec-review) that bubbles.validate cannot author. Routing next to bubbles.docs.

Security review of changed surfaces in internal/whatsapp/assistant_adapter/* (adapter.go, webhook_handler.go, verify_test.go). Threat model: webhook spoofing, signature forgery, replay/oversize/DoS, secret leakage, identity bypass. Findings: NONE. (1) HMAC-SHA256 over RAW request body using crypto/hmac.Equal (constant-time) — implemented in HMACVerifier.Verify; signature prefix validated, hex-decoded, length-checked via hmac.Equal which rejects length mismatches. (2) Verify happens BEFORE JSON parse and BEFORE identity resolution in serveDelivery — correct order; no parser exposure to unsigned bytes. (3) verify_token comparison in VerifyChallenge uses hmac.Equal (constant-time) — no timing oracle on the GET subscribe handshake. (4) Body capped at 1 MiB via http.MaxBytesReader with explicit MaxBytesError → 413; protects against memory-exhaustion DoS. (5) Missing signature → 401 missing_signature; malformed/mismatch → 401 invalid_signature; identity unknown → refused with metric, no info disclosure. (6) NewWebhookHandler panics on nil Adapter (fail-loud per smackerel-no-defaults). (7) NewAdapter / Verify require AppSecret and VerifyToken non-empty — no silent disable. (8) No raw request body content logged on any path — only structured envelope (kind, reason, remote_ip, hashed user id once resolved). (9) Idempotency tracked by TransportMessageID so Meta retries cannot double-dispatch. (10) Only hardcoded secret in tree is test fixture testAppSecret in verify_test.go (test-only, allowed). OWASP mapping: A01 broken access control (mitigated: signature-before-identity-before-dispatch); A02 cryptographic failures (mitigated: HMAC-SHA256 + hmac.Equal); A05 misconfiguration (mitigated: panic/error on missing secret/verifier); A09 logging failures (mitigated: no body content logged). Verdict: SECURE. No routing required.

Diagnostic stabilization pass over internal/whatsapp/assistant_adapter/** (webhook_handler.go, idempotency.go). 5 findings appended to report.md under 'Stabilization Findings — bubbles.stabilize 2026-06-02'. S-072-1 INFRASTRUCTURE/HIGH-conditional: in-process idempotency cache (IdempotencyCacheCapacity=16384, FIFO) explicitly assumes single-replica WhatsApp ingress; horizontal scale-out would break at-most-once delivery under Meta retries — recommend either pinning replica=1 in deploy/compose with a compose_contract_test assertion or promoting dedup to a shared store (Postgres short-TTL row on wamid or Redis SET with TTL). S-072-2 CONFIG/MEDIUM: WebhookMaxBodyBytes=1<<20 and IdempotencyCacheCapacity=16384 are hardcoded constants violating SST zero-defaults policy (gate G028) — recommend SST keys ASSISTANT_TRANSPORTS_WHATSAPP_WEBHOOK_MAX_BODY_BYTES and ..._IDEMPOTENCY_CAPACITY. S-072-3 RELIABILITY/MEDIUM: webhook ACK is blocked on synchronous facade Handle + Cloud-API send; under load this could trigger Meta retries — recommend either add assistant_whatsapp_webhook_latency_seconds histogram for SLO visibility or defer user-facing send to a goroutine after capture-as-fallback persistence. S-072-4 OBSERVABILITY/LOW: handler has counters and per-request INFO logs with latency_ms but no Prometheus histogram — recommend adding outcome-labeled latency histogram. S-072-5 PERF/INFO: on a duplicate Meta retry, Translate (identity DB lookup) runs before MarkSeen short-circuits — cheap micro-optimization to extract wamid before Translate, only worth doing alongside S-072-1 or S-072-3. Zero CRITICAL, 1 HIGH-conditional, 2 MEDIUM, 1 LOW, 1 INFO. Zero inline fixes. Routed work: S-072-1 and S-072-2 should reach bubbles.plan before any horizontal-scaling decision or production hardening pass; S-072-3/4/5 are operational improvements that can land later. Domains audited: performance, infrastructure, configuration, reliability, resource-usage, build/CI. No build or test regressions observed during inspection. No scope DoD flips; no certification change. status remains in_progress (workflow-ceiling reasons unchanged from prior bubbles.validate run — docs/audit/chaos/spec-review still pending). Claim Source: code-inspection only; no runtime measurement, load test, or chaos run performed in this session.

Simplify pass over recently changed files in internal/whatsapp/assistant_adapter/** (adapter.go 636 LOC, webhook_handler.go 338 LOC, render.go 421 LOC, idempotency.go 94 LOC, mount.go 64 LOC). Two minimal, behavior-preserving fixes applied: (1) adapter.go — extracted resolveUserID(ctx, fromPhone) helper to dedupe identical 5-line phone-hash + identity.Resolve sequence shared by Translate() and Identity(); net -3 LOC. (2) webhook_handler.go — removed dead variable initialization in serveDelivery signature-rejection branch (`reason := "mismatch"` was unconditionally overwritten in the immediately following if/else); collapsed to `reason := "malformed"` plus single if-override; net -3 LOC. Verification: go build ./internal/whatsapp/assistant_adapter/ ./internal/assistant/intenttrace/ RC=0; go test ./internal/whatsapp/assistant_adapter/ ./internal/assistant/intenttrace/ RC=0 (both packages green; whatsapp_adapter 0.760s, intenttrace 0.051s). Findings NOT applied (rationale): (a) RenderToPhone duplicates `cloudSendTotal.WithLabelValues(...)` and `renderErrors.WithLabelValues('cloud_api')` increments in text/interactive branches, but the switch differentiates the cloud method call and consolidating would obscure rather than clarify the dispatch; (b) SetTransportStatus has trivial bool→gauge if/else pattern (4 lines) — extracting a helper would be net-neutral; (c) render.go EncodeConfirmPayload / EncodeDisambigPayload / EncodeResetPayload share a tiny prefix-concat shape but each has distinct decoder semantics, not worth abstracting. Overall verdict: surface is well-structured per spec 072 design, with minimal over-engineering. Net LOC: -6. No regressions. certification.status unchanged (in_progress; ceiling-bound by full-delivery workflow). Claim Source: executed (build RC=0, tests RC=0 captured this session).

This audit does not bypass remaining specialist phases. Spot-Check Recommendations: (i) manually verify a sampled TP-072 e2e row (e.g. ./smackerel.sh test e2e --go-run '^TestWhatsAppRoundtripE2E$') against a fresh disposable stack; (ii) confirm bubbles.docs updates docs/API.md and docs/Operations.md for the WhatsApp transport surface; (iii) confirm S-072-2 (SST zero-defaults gap on WebhookMaxBodyBytes and IdempotencyCacheCapacity) is captured in a planning packet — this is a NO-DEFAULTS policy gap, not just an operational nit.

Facade-level chaos pass against the WhatsApp Business transport surface. Added internal/whatsapp/assistant_adapter/chaos_072_test.go with two seeded-PRNG fuzz tests: (1) TestChaos072_WebhookHandler_NeverPanicsAndStaysIn4xx5xxClosedSet throws 200 random HTTP requests at NewWebhookHandler — random methods (POST/GET/PUT/DELETE/PATCH), random body shapes (nil, empty, invalid JSON, oversize >1 MiB, valid sample, random unicode/control bytes), random X-Hub-Signature-256 values (missing, wrong-prefix, non-hex, all-a, wrong-secret, legitimate, garbage), and random GET hub.mode/hub.verify_token/hub.challenge combinations — and asserts no panic and that every response status is in the closed vocabulary {200, 400, 401, 403, 405, 413}; (2) TestChaos072_Render_NeverPanicsForRandomResponseShapes fires 200 random AssistantResponse shapes through Render at random maxTextChars in {1, 10, 100, 1024, 4096, 8192} and asserts no panic, Kind ∈ {text, interactive_buttons, interactive_list} on success, and Text body length stays bounded. Verification: go test ./internal/whatsapp/assistant_adapter/ -run TestChaos072 -count=1 -v -timeout 90s RC=0 (0.253s wall). Seeds (logged for reproducibility): chaos-072 webhook seed=1780373200973677796, chaos-072 render seed=1780373201199913153. Findings: ZERO P0/P1/P2/P3/P4 — the webhook handler kept its status vocabulary closed under 200 random inputs with the facade never invoked for an unverified delivery, and Render kept its closed-vocabulary output union under 200 random shapes. No bug artifacts created. certification.status unchanged (full-delivery ceiling already at validate); this chaos pass satisfies the bubbles.chaos workflow gate. Claim Source: executed.

Once-before-done spec-review pass for spec 072 (WhatsApp Business Webhook Adapter). Audited spec.md (401 LOC), design.md (325 LOC), scopes.md (400 LOC), report.md (753 LOC), scenario-manifest.json (10 scenarios SCN-072-A01..A10), test-plan.json (14 TP rows), uservalidation.md (12 LOC) against the shipped implementation in internal/whatsapp/assistant_adapter/** (adapter.go, webhook_handler.go, render.go, idempotency.go, mount.go, capture_test.go, roundtrip_idempotency_test.go, verify_test.go, template_policy_test.go, render_golden_test.go, chaos_072_test.go) plus tests/integration/assistant/whatsapp_*.go, tests/integration/assistant/transport_*.go, tests/e2e/assistant/whatsapp_*.go, and internal/config/assistant_whatsapp_test.go. (1) File-existence check: every implementation path referenced in design.md and report.md is present on disk; no moved or renamed files. (2) Contract alignment: SST keys ASSISTANT_TRANSPORTS_WHATSAPP_* in spec match internal/config validators; webhook path, signature header, and Cloud-API render table in design.md match adapter.go + render.go + webhook_handler.go. (3) Scenario coverage: 10 BDD scenarios map 1:1 to 14 TP test rows; bubbles.audit recorded traceability-guard RC=0 with 10/10 DoD fidelity. (4) Evidence integrity: bubbles.validate logged all 14 rows PASS on disposable stack; bubbles.test re-ran with executed evidence; bubbles.security verdict SECURE (zero findings); bubbles.chaos seeded fuzz tests PASS (chaos_072_test.go committed); bubbles.simplify net -6 LOC with build+tests RC=0; bubbles.audit ⚠️ SHIP_WITH_NOTES on artifact/traceability axis; bubbles.regression confirmed disjoint footprint vs spec 071. (5) Redundancy/superseded-truth check: report.md contains BLOCKED entries from 2026-06-01 sessions that are superseded by the 2026-06-02 CERTIFIED DONE session — these are clearly framed as historical context with explicit "superseded by" routing references; not stale active truth. (6) Deferral-language scan: only one hit in spec.md §8 (Open Questions) marking outbound media and message templates as out-of-scope for v1 — by-design forward-planning, not stale deferral. (7) Stabilize findings S-072-1 (single-replica idempotency cache) and S-072-2 (hardcoded WebhookMaxBodyBytes / IdempotencyCacheCapacity violating SST zero-defaults) remain open routing items for bubbles.plan but do NOT invalidate the shipped contract or block done — they are forward-looking hardening for multi-replica scale-out. Trust classification: CURRENT. Drift verdict: zero MAJOR_DRIFT or OBSOLETE findings; spec accurately reflects implementation reality.
